summary: Epic Scarlet>Fire and flawless Playing‑Band > Wheel sequence
blurb: The second set erupts with a monster Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue into an unbroken Playing In The Band → The Wheel → China Doll marathon. Jerry’s guitar soars while Brent’s keyboards add punch, and the first set holds strong with Row Jimmy and Lazy Lightning → Supplication.
review: May 11, 1981 at Veterans Memorial Coliseum delivered one of the spring‑’81 high‑water marks for the Dead. The first set rolled solidly through New Minglewood Blues, Sugaree and a tight Lazy Lightning → Supplication pair, but it was the second set that truly ignited the night. A blistering Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain launch cascaded straight into a seamless Playing In The Band → Drums → Space → The Wheel → Playing In The Band → China Doll suite that many fans describe as “monster‑tight” and “warp‑speed space noodles.” Highlights continued with C.C. Rider, an emotive To Lay Me Down, and a rousing encore of (I Can’t Get No) Satisfaction. Jerry Garcia’s tone was called perfect, his solos vivid and on top of everything, while Brent Mydland settled into an integral keyboard role, delivering “sick” C.C. Rider lines. The rhythm section stayed locked—Mickey Hart’s drumming tight through the drums/space segment and Bill Kreutzmann kept the pulse driving—though Bob Weir’s vocal forays on Around & Around drew some criticism. Overall, the show balances high‑energy jams with melodic storytelling, making it a must‑listen snapshot of the band in prime form.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five of six recordings are positive, praising the second‑set jams, Jerry's guitar work and overall sound; one mixed review notes occasional patchy moments but does not outweigh the strong consensus.

best_recording.reason: Excellent matrix mix with clear instrument separation, deep ambience and no drop‑outs, capturing the full power of the second‑set jams.

key_highlights: Monster Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue into a seamless jam
Flawless Playing In The Band → The Wheel → China Doll sequence
Standout To Lay Me Down and C.C. Rider performances
Tight first‑set energy with Row Jimmy and Lazy Lightning → Supplication

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s tone was perfect and his solos were vivid, energetic and often described as the MVP of the night, especially on Scarlet > Fire and the Playing In The Band outro.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provided a solid, driving bass foundation with notable riffs that propelled the jams forward.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s vocals were uneven; his delivery on Around & Around was called odd and boring by several listeners.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland settled into an integral keyboard role, delivering sick solos on C.C. Rider and adding punch throughout the second set.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s drumming stayed tight, especially during the drums/space section leading into The Wheel.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann provided solid rhythmic support, contributing to the strong interplay that kept the jams flowing.
